title: Freedom as the Purpose of Society: Historical and Contemporary ...
content: Introduction:Freedomhas long been a cherished ideal in human societies. Philosophers throughout history have debated whetherfreedomis the central purpose ofsociety- in other words, whether the highest aim of a political community is to secure and expand the liberty of its members. From the ancient visions of Plato and Aristotle, through the social contract theorists like Hobbes, Locke ...
